description: Foundational charter outlining project purpose, scope, objectives, governance, roles, high-level milestones, and approvals to formally authorize proceeding with detailed planning.
steps_to_create: Articulate project purpose, scope boundaries, and success criteria
Identify key stakeholders and establish governance roles
Outline high-level budget, timeline, and milestones
Capture initial risks, dependencies, and constraints
Define approval authorities and escalation paths
Obtain initial sign-off from sponsor (Producer/Director)
approval_authorities: Producer; Director; Executive Producer; Legal Counsel

description: High-level framework for optimizing production efficiency across traditional, hybrid, and lean production approaches, including resource allocation, cost controls, and performance metrics.
steps_to_create: Assess current production workflows and cost structure
Define target efficiency scenarios (Traditional, Hybrid, Lean)
Establish KPI set and monitoring mechanisms
Identify critical path, dependencies, and risk mitigations
Align production approach with Talent and Distribution strategies
Secure governance and sign-off for chosen approach

description: High-level framework guiding how the original story will be adapted for a modern Hong Kong setting, including twist strategy, tonal direction, core themes, IP alignment, and censorship considerations.
steps_to_create: Define narrative goals and core themes
Evaluate twist options and their impact on audience expectations
Map alignment with IP rights and Hong Kong setting
Assess censorship risk and prepare alternative endings
Outline sign-off process with stakeholders
Document creative boundaries and escalation paths
approval_authorities: Producer; Director; Legal Counsel; Geopolitical Risk Assessor

description: High-level framework governing actor and director selection, casting process, and talent management to balance international appeal with authenticity and cost.
steps_to_create: Define target talent profiles (lead/ensemble)
Create shortlists and scouting plan
Plan auditions and screen tests
Evaluate alignment with Hong Kong identity and budget
Negotiate with agents and manage scheduling
Obtain sign-off from Producer/Director

description: High-level framework to identify, assess, and mitigate geopolitical and censorship risks impacting production and distribution in Hong Kong and regional markets.
steps_to_create: Develop risk register and scoring for political/censorship factors
Define mitigation options (narrative adjustments, permits, versions)
Plan for permits and market access strategies
Establish reporting/escalation processes to Producer/Director
Identify external counsel and censorship experts
Create scenario-based contingency plans
approval_authorities: Producer; Director; Legal Counsel; Censorship Expert

description: High-level framework detailing the release architecture across theatrical, VOD, and streaming platforms, including timing, sequencing, regional considerations, and revenue targets.
steps_to_create: Define release windows (theatrical, VOD, streaming) and sequencing
Map regional strategy and platform priorities
Align with marketing, production readiness, and currency plan
Identify KPI targets for each channel
Prepare high-level revenue projections and risk adjustments
Obtain sign-off from Producer and Finance Lead

document_name: Baseline Assessment: Current State of IP Rights & Censorship
description: Baseline assessment to identify current status of IP rights, potential restrictions, and censorship risks affecting planning.
responsible_role_type: Legal Counsel / IP Rights Acquisition Specialist
steps_to_create: Gather current rights status and ownership details
Identify gaps and potential legal/administrative obstacles
Document regulatory restrictions and censorship considerations
Propose mitigation actions and quick wins
Align with project timeline and approvals

description: Drafted high-level milestones and timeline to guide planning and gating reviews.
steps_to_create: List major project phases (IP rights, script, casting, permits, filming, post, delivery)
Identify gating reviews and decision points
Estimate duration and buffers
Align with budgets and risk plan
Get sign-off from Producer/Director

description: Plan for initial market research and audience analysis to inform strategy and messaging.
steps_to_create: Define research questions and target segments
Identify data sources and methods (surveys, focus groups, industry data)
Outline budget, timeline, and deliverables
Establish success metrics and reporting cadence
Obtain approvals from Producer/Director
